Root Cause: The function shouldTraverse is not correctly handling the case where a function is being lent to a prototype using the dojo.declare function.

Suggestion 1: Modify the shouldTraverse function to correctly handle the dojo.declare case
The shouldTraverse function needs to be modified to correctly handle the case where a function is being lent to a prototype using the dojo.declare function. This can be done by adding a check for the dojo.declare function and returning true if it is found.

Suggestion 2: Add a special case for the dojo.declare function
A special case can be added to the shouldTraverse function to handle the dojo.declare function. This can be done by checking if the parent node is a call to the dojo.declare function and returning true if it is.

Suggestion 3: Modify the shouldTraverse function to use a more general approach
Instead of adding special cases for specific functions, the shouldTraverse function can be modified to use a more general approach. This can be done by checking if the function is being lent to a prototype and returning true if it is. This approach would handle not only the dojo.declare function but also other similar cases.
